reachability.evidence: Direct HTTP probes against api.yeswehack.com return 200 with valid JSON when Authorization: Bearer <jwt> is provided. No Cloudflare/Vercel/DataDome challenge observed. CORS preflight (OPTIONS) returns 200. Plain net/http transport in the printed CLI is sufficient.

auth.notes: JWT is stored in localStorage.access_token on yeswehack.com. The printed CLI ships an 'auth login --chrome' command that reads it from the user's Chrome profile so they do not have to copy-paste from DevTools. Refresh path is documented at apps.yeswehack.com/oauth/v2/token. PATs are out of scope because researchers cannot generate them.

rate_limiting.recommendation: Use adaptive limiter (cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter). Start at 1 req/s, back off on 429, raise on sustained success.

code_of_conduct_constraint: YesWeHack platform recently published a Code of Conduct that explicitly calls out program spamming and AI slop. The printed CLI must NOT enable bulk-submit, template-flood, or any 'submit N reports at once' workflow. Submit/draft commands are guarded with dry-run defaults and explicit confirmation, no batch mode.
